name: Homemade Lamb Doner Kebab
description: A simple and delicious homemade lamb doner kebab that will truly surprise you with its authentic restaurant-style flavor! Juicy, flavorful, and perfectly seasoned, this dish is a Middle Eastern classic you can make right in your own kitchen.

steps: In a large mixing bowl, add: Minced lamb (about 500 grams).
Then add minced beef, also 500 grams.
Next, grated onion, around 250 grams for flavor and moisture.
Add one-fourth cup of yogurt to keep the meat soft and juicy.
Then one tablespoon of grated garlic. Now for the spices: one tablespoon each of paprika, cumin, and dried parsley, plus one teaspoon each of salt and black pepper.
And half a teaspoon each of cinnamon, ginger, cardamom, and cloves for that perfect Middle Eastern aroma.
Mix everything really well until all the flavors come together beautifully.
Now spread a portion of the meat evenly on a sheet of parchment paper.
Roll the parchment sheet tightly with the meat inside to form a log.
Place your rolled kebab logs in an oven-safe dish.
Bake them in a preheated oven at 450°F (250°C) for about 15 minutes.
Once baked, pour out any excess juices from the dish.
Unroll the kebab logs carefully onto a baking tray.
Turn on the oven broiler and broil for up to 5 minutes, just until golden.
Brush the top with melted butter for that perfect shine and flavor.
Now break the kebab into small, thin slices — just like at the restaurant!
Time to make the creamy sauce! In a small bowl, mix together: Half a cup of yogurt (about 120 grams).
Add half a cup of mayonnaise, that’s 120 grams too.
Then one teaspoon dried parsley and one-fourth teaspoon black pepper.
Add salt to taste.
Finally, a little grated garlic (about 5 grams. Mix it well).
Now let’s build our doner sandwich! Spread a good layer of the sauce on fresh pita bread.
Add sliced pickles for a tangy crunch.
Then add the doner kebab.
Top it with sliced onions and fresh tomatoes.
A drizzle of hot sauce for some heat,
And finally, crisp lettuce for freshness.

notes: You can prepare the meat ahead of time and freeze it for later. Try using only lamb for a richer, more traditional flavor. Adjust the spice level to your liking by adding more or less hot sauce.
